title: Programming Paradigms: OOP and Functional
description: How procedural, object-oriented and functional programming approach the same problems differently, covering classes, inheritance, polymorphism, pure functions, recursion and higher-order functions.
objectives: Compare procedural, object-oriented and functional paradigms and explain what kinds of problem each suits
Define a class with attributes, methods and an `__init__` constructor, and explain encapsulation
Use inheritance to extend a superclass and override a method, and explain polymorphism
Write a pure function and explain why immutability and no side effects make code easier to reason about
Write a recursive function with a correct base case and recursive case, and trace its call stack
Use higher-order functions such as `map` and `filter` to process data without explicit loops
